description: News segment from KnoxNews.com featuring University of Tennessee Hospital's Center for Advanced Medical Simulation. The video shows a tour of the simulation facility with instructors demonstrating use of high-fidelity patient simulators (mannequins) for training healthcare professionals. One instructor demonstrates airway management on a mannequin head, and another provides oral explanation of simulation training methods.
why_not_scored: This is not an FLS (Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery) task video. The content shows: (1) No laparoscopic instruments or trainer boxes visible; (2) High-fidelity full-body and head simulators used for general clinical skills, not FLS-specific tasks; (3) Narrated educational/news segment with multiple speakers and facility tour; (4) No peg transfer, pattern cutting, endoloop, or suture tasks; (5) Demonstrates clinical assessment and airway management, not surgical technical skills assessed by FLS rubrics.

demonstrated_techniques: High-fidelity patient simulator use for training
verbal_cues: Instructors explaining simulation center capabilities and training methodologies
teaching_content.context: Medical simulation facility tour and training overview

confidence_rationale: Content is clearly a news/educational segment showing a medical simulation facility, not an FLS task. No ambiguity regarding classification.
recommendations: If you have an actual FLS task video (peg transfer, pattern cut, endoloop, extracorporeal suture, or intracorporeal suture), please provide that for scoring.
